studies.title: We Gave Six LLMs a Family to Run. Most Hesitated. One Crossed the Safety Line.
studies.scope: A production-shaped restraint study on synthetic household scenarios; no real user data was used.
studies.method: Six OpenRouter model identifiers each routed 227 scenarios three times at temperature 0.2 through a forced do/ask/confirm/chat choice. Routing was scored deterministically against an answer key built from a three-model panel.
limitations: The scenario set is small and hand-written by the product founder.
The forced-choice format likely inflates asking relative to open conversation.
Two panel judges were also models under test, and Gemini’s apparent rank depended materially on its own-family judge.
Only two tested identifiers were date-pinned snapshots; provider routing and model behavior can change.

studies.title: We Asked 8 LLMs to Run Our Family’s Life. Two Tried to Book a Vacation.
studies.scope: An April 2026 production case study using Honeydew’s prompt and a small synthetic scenario set.
studies.method: Eight models were tested on 35 scenarios across six categories, ten trials per model-scenario pair.
limitations: The prompt was built around GPT-family models, creating prompt-coupling the study cannot separate from capability.
The June 24 restraint study supersedes the original noisy-input and duplicate-handling conclusions.
This is a production case study, not peer-reviewed research or a universal model ranking.
The repository documents a dated production case study; it does not turn the result into a universal model ranking.

studies.title: Do LLMs Actually Cite Your Startup? A 90-Day Field Note
studies.scope: A 90-day descriptive field note on one early-stage consumer product’s observable LLM referrals and one ten-query Perplexity citation snapshot.
studies.method: GA4 source-attributed sessions and a separate browser-referrer event were kept as different units. Ten queries were run once each in fresh Perplexity sessions and checked separately in ordinary web search.
limitations: There was no pre-intervention baseline or control group, so the field note cannot establish causal lift.
GA4 sessions and custom referrer events are different units and must not be divided into a capture-rate estimate.
The citation check used one provider, one product, ten queries, and one run per query.
Typed URLs, later searches, cross-device discovery, and other indirect journeys are outside the observed data.
